The Observation class — Holding an observation array¶

New in version 0.1.

Module author: Claude Gibert <claude.gibert@synopticview.co.uk>

class observations.Observation(object)¶

Public methods¶

Observation(value = None, info = None):
Parameters:
  • value (array, numpy array, sequence) – a indexed variable holding observation values
  • info (any type) – additional information stored in the object

Creates an Observation instance.

values():

returns the array of observations

info():

return the information passed when instanciating the object as is.

__call__(value == None):
Parameters:value (ObsFilter or an array of Booleans) – selects the observations where a True value is stored. The parameter value must have the same shape as the array held by this instance.

returns a one-dimensional array containing all values matching True values. If value is not given, returns the array of observations, just like the values method.

Other methods¶

Observation implements all numerical and logical operators. It also delegates all unknown calls to the variable it holds. If initialised with a numpy array:

import numpy as np
from semperpy.observations.observation import Observation

a = np.arange(12)
o = Observation(a)
print o.shape
  -->(12,)
print o.mean()
  -->5.5

The shape attribute and the mean method are delegated to numpy.
